description: Subsystem manifest for the wiki generator. Each entry defines a coherent module that spans multiple files. Hand-maintained for V1.

subsystems.name: MessageBroker
subsystems.description: Central event hub routing messages between terminals, caching tasks/terminals/profiles, firing events to UI panels, and delivering webhooks.

subsystems.name: TaskDatabase
subsystems.description: SQLite persistence for the kanban system. 21+ tables covering tasks, checklists, helpers, inbox, attachments, activity, summaries, and session tracking.

subsystems.name: MainForm
subsystems.description: WinForms application shell. Hosts docked panels, orchestrates terminal lifecycle, wires MessageBroker events to UI, manages theme and layout.

subsystems.name: REST API
subsystems.description: HTTP server exposing ~161 endpoints across 26 controllers on port 5050. Bridges MCP tools and UI panels to MessageBroker and databases.

subsystems.name: Code Graph
subsystems.description: Roslyn-based C# code indexer and query layer. Extracts symbols (classes, methods, properties) and relationships (calls, inherits, references) into cg_* SQLite tables.

subsystems.name: Session Lineage
subsystems.description: Tracks Claude Code session history, lineage chains (parent/child spawn relationships), and semantic session memory via embeddings + FTS5 search.

subsystems.name: Knowledge Database
subsystems.description: Stores knowledge entries (patterns, decisions, gotchas), code digests, and research cache. FTS5 search across knowledge content.

subsystems.name: MCP Server
subsystems.description: Node.js MCP tool definitions (88 tools) that bridge Claude Code agents to MultiTerminal's REST API. Lives outside the repo at %APPDATA%/multiterminal/mcp but managed by McpConfigService.

subsystems.name: WebView2 Panel Pattern
subsystems.description: Pattern for building UI panels hosted in WebView2. Each panel has an outer DockContent + inner UserControl + HTML/CSS/JS, wired to MessageBroker events.

subsystems.name: Terminal Management
subsystems.description: Terminal spawning, ConPty lifecycle, agent process coordination, and companion process management.
